name: Apricot Frangipane Tart
recipeIngredient: 1 recipe pate sèche, rolled to a thickness of 1/8 inch (2.5 mm), fitted into a tart pan with a removable bottom
1 stick (113 g) unsalted butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the edge of the dough with the rolling pin.
2. Prick the shell with a fork and chill the shell for 30 minutes.
3. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) and preheat the baking sheet.
4. In a bowl fitted with a whisk, cream together the butter and 1/4 cup sugar until light and fluffy.
5. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition, then add the ground almonds and vanilla extract. Mix until fully incorporated.
6. Spread the frangipane mixture into the chilled shell.
7. Arrange the apricot halves with their cut sides up over the filling.
8. Bake the tart on the preheated baking sheet in the lower third of the oven for 40 minutes, or until the filling is golden and set.
9. Preheat the broiler.
10. Sprinkle the remaining 2 teaspoons sugar over the top and place the tart under the hot broiler about 6 inches from the heat for 2 to 3 minutes, or until the filling is caramelized.
11. Brush the tart with the apricot glaze, let it cool completely, then remove the rim of the pan.
Yield: 1 tart
